description: A continuation course for learners who have completed Kafka Basics, covering operations, reliability, integrations, and stream processing. You will design high availability and durability using replication and ISR, understand delivery guarantees such as at-least-once and exactly-once along with idempotent producers, control ordering guarantees and load distribution through partitioning strategies and custom partitioners, manage schema evolution with Schema Registry and Avro, integrate external systems without code using Kafka Connect, get started with stateful stream processing via Kafka Streams, and round out your practical skills with retention, log compaction, and essential operations and monitoring.
summary: A continuation course for learners who have completed Kafka Basics. Topics include replication and ISR, delivery guarantees and idempotent producers, partitioning strategies, Schema Registry, Kafka Connect, Kafka Streams, retention, log compaction, and operations and monitoring fundamentals.
target_audience: Learners who have completed Kafka Basics (messaging fundamentals, Topic/Partition/Offset/Broker, architecture/ZooKeeper vs KRaft, Producer basics/acks/keys, Consumer/Consumer Groups, local end-to-end lab). Intermediate learners looking to expand their skills in operations, reliability, integrations, and stream processing.

lectures.title: Replication and ISR — The Foundation of High Availability and Durability
objectives: Understand how the leader-follower replication model and ISR (In-Sync Replicas) guarantee data durability
Explain how the combination of replication.factor, min.insync.replicas, and acks affects availability and durability
Create a topic with a higher replication factor, force-stop a broker, and directly observe leader election and failure recovery

lectures.title: Delivery Semantics and Idempotent Producers
objectives: Distinguish between at-most-once, at-least-once, and exactly-once delivery guarantees and their trade-offs
Understand the duplicate message problem caused by retries and how idempotent producers prevent it
Describe the end-to-end flow of exactly-once processing using transactions and read_committed

lectures.title: Partitioning Strategies and Keys — Ordering Guarantees and Load Distribution
objectives: Understand how key-hash-based partition assignment guarantees ordering for messages with the same key
Analyze the default partitioners (key-hash and sticky) and the hot partition / data skew problem
Implement a custom partitioner using the Partitioner interface to directly control routing rules

lectures.title: Serialization and Schema Registry — Avro and Schema Evolution
objectives: Understand the need for message serialization and the advantages of schema-based formats like Avro over JSON
Describe how Schema Registry centrally manages schemas and enforces compatibility checks
Register an Avro schema, practice schema evolution by adding a field, and verify compatibility rules

lectures.title: Kafka Connect — Integrating External Systems Without Code
objectives: Understand the role of Kafka Connect in building data pipelines with Source and Sink connectors, without writing application code
Explain the relationship between Standalone and Distributed modes, and between connectors, tasks, and workers
Register and configure a connector via the REST API to build a data flow between an external system and Kafka

lectures.title: Kafka Streams Basics — Stream Processing and Stateful Aggregation
objectives: Understand the stream processing model that Kafka Streams provides, compared to writing direct Consumer logic
Distinguish between KStream and KTable, and between stream operations such as map, filter, groupBy, and count
Write and run a simple Streams application that performs stateful aggregation

lectures.title: Retention, Log Compaction, and Operations and Monitoring Fundamentals
objectives: Understand the difference and use cases of time/size-based retention (delete) versus log compaction (compact), which keeps only the latest value per key
Explain what key operational metrics such as Consumer Lag, throughput, and ISR changes mean in practice
Apply retention and compaction policies to a topic and directly inspect key metrics and Consumer Lag
